title: Zephyranthes lineata
intro: Zephyranthes lineata, also known as the 'Silver Rainbow Lily' or 'Mexican Zephyr Lily', is a species of flowering plant in the family Amaryllidaceae, characterized by its white, striped flowers and long, narrow leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds, infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Zephyranthes lineata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared into infusions, teas, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin irritations.
taxonomy: Zephyranthes lineata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Amaryllidaceae. Within this family, it belongs to the subfamily Amaryllidoideae. The genus Zephyranthes is a group of plants commonly known as the fairy lilies or zephyr lilies. Zephyranthes lineata is native to Mexico and Central America. This plant is often cultivated for its showy white flowers with a delicate, lily-like appearance.
uses: Zephyranthes lineata is used in traditional medicine in various regions, particularly in Latin America, where it is employed to treat various health conditions. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. In folk medicine, Zephyranthes lineata is used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Additionally, it has been used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis due to its anti-inflammatory properties. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal plant.

side_effects: Zephyranthes lineata, also known as the striped zephyr lily, is a species of flowering plant native to Central and South America.
